sources.name: Athanor canonical implementation root
notes: Only implementation-authority root.
Landing zone for all accepted imports and ecosystem control artifacts.

sources.name: Athanor GitHub publication line
notes: Remote mainline.
Local publication work must align with origin/main before pushing.

sources.name: DEV runtime authority repo
notes: Runtime authority only.
Governed by runtime-ownership packets, not implementation absorption.

sources.name: Athanor next-gen incubation root
notes: Mandatory harvest lane.
Selective import only; freeze after useful value is absorbed.
The first continuity-doc slice landed in Athanor as PROJECT.md and docs/CODEX-NEXT-STEPS.md on 2026-04-06.
The vault-ssh helper variant has now been explicitly rejected as weaker than Athanor's current topology-backed implementation.
The old FastMCP agent-server bridge is also now governed as lineage residue rather than a live import candidate.

sources.name: Reconcile evidence workspace
notes: Mandatory harvest lane for GitHub governance, artifact triage, and reconciliation findings.
Archive evidence, not a competing authority root.
The deployment helper trio render_ansible_template.py, Invoke-DeploymentDriftAudit.ps1, and Invoke-RepoDeploymentManifestAudit.ps1 has now been imported into Athanor as maintained tooling.

The remaining GitHub, portfolio, normalization, and recurring wrapper scripts are now governed as lineage/reference only because Athanor has native discovery, preservation, maturity, and validator-backed guardrail surfaces.

sources.name: Local-System lineage repo
notes: Major predecessor architecture line.
Deep subsystem review required before any import.
The memory, mind, MCP-server, shared-model, workspace-catalog, and capability-router seams have now all been demoted to lineage/reference only unless a narrower missing seam is identified later.

sources.name: local-system-v4 lineage reference
notes: Shallow reference lane around Local-System.
Use only for deltas not already represented elsewhere.

sources.name: Agentic Coding Tools primary local root
notes: Primary shared-module candidate.
Chosen as the primary review root over the portfolio clone because both clones share the same observed head commit and this root carries the richer local residue.
Review route registry, event ingest, route auditor, command center API, configgen, agent_hook, and athanor-artifacts.

The route_registry, event_ingest, route_auditor, command_center_api, agent_hook, configgen, and athanor-artifact contract lanes are now all demoted to lineage/reference only; the imported Codex worktree tooling did not come from this repo.

sources.name: Agentic Coding Tools portfolio clone
notes: Duplicate clone of the same repo.
Compared against the primary root and retained as the cleaner preservation duplicate.
Do not use as the active inspection root while the richer root-level clone remains under review.

sources.name: AI Dev Control Plane repo
notes: Reviewed from live local state on 2026-04-07.
Repo-local contract keeps it local-first and sample-data driven.
Treat as operator-tooling lineage and possible dashboard-fixture reference, not a live shared-module extraction target.

sources.name: Codex System Config
notes: Selective operator-tooling mine.
Do not collapse machine-level Codex control-plane concerns into Athanor core.
Only the worktree-lane scripts are currently worth Athanor-native import; the scaffolder, Codex-home audits, rollout audits, and repo templates remain operator-tooling reference outside Athanor core.

sources.name: Development docs stash
notes: Document mine only.
Promote verified material; do not treat as active truth.
The reviewed architecture-decision, reconciliation-summary, build-manifest, and Claude-bootstrap artifacts are now all governed as lineage or historical reference only, not pending active import.

sources.name: Development reference stash
notes: Research mine only.
Use for MCP, provider, model, and tooling context where it improves current Athanor decisions.
The reviewed model-selection, CLI-tool, MCP-server, and AI-tools notes are governed as external research only and do not override current registry-backed provider or tooling truth.

sources.name: Field Inspect primary local root
notes: Primary local Field Inspect workspace discovered by the three-depth C drive sweep.
Live product with active VAULT deployment posture and ongoing local changes.
Tenant-family audit now confirms the primary root is the git-backed workspace on branch codex/perpetual-coo-loop with six sibling git worktree lanes.
Sibling C:/Field Inspect-* roots are active git worktree lanes that should be preserved and reviewed for merge-or-freeze decisions before any collapse.
The surviving operations-runtime lane is now governed by the dedicated replay packet and generated report under docs/operations and reports/reconciliation.
Use the generated tenant-family audit and replay packet report for live branch state and execution posture instead of relying on prose snapshots.
The safe operations-runtime tranche plus its minimum support set was replayed into C:/Field Inspect on 2026-04-07 with npm run typecheck and the packet target tests green in the authority root.

sources.name: Field Inspect mobile followup lane
notes: Sibling Field Inspect working lane discovered by the C drive sweep.
Tenant-family audit confirms this is a clean git worktree lane on branch codex/reconcile-mobile-followup tracking Dirty13itch/Field_Inspect.
Ahead count is zero versus the primary root lineage, so this is now a freeze-first lane rather than an active replay candidate.

sources.name: Field Inspect operations runtime lane
notes: Sibling Field Inspect working lane focused on operations/runtime work.
Tenant-family audit confirms this is a clean git worktree lane on branch codex/reconcile-operations-runtime tracking Dirty13itch/Field_Inspect.
This is the only sibling lane that remains an active selective replay candidate because it carries real operations runtime product code, tests, and dashboards.
Direct wholesale merge remains disallowed; replay should happen as targeted cherry-picks governed by the dedicated packet and generated report.
The replay order, live execution posture, and acceptance commands are now captured in the dedicated Field Inspect operations-runtime replay packet.

A dedicated review worktree at C:/Field Inspect-ops-safe-review now carries the safe operations tranche plus the minimum support-set needed for contract-coherent validation; npm run typecheck and the targeted replay tests passed there on 2026-04-07.

The lane now has one executed outcome in the authority root: the safe operations-runtime tranche is landed and green in C:/Field Inspect, so the remaining lane scope is the shared-project/report-delivery second tranche plus freeze/reclassification follow-through.

sources.name: Field Inspect platform ops lane
notes: Sibling Field Inspect working lane focused on platform operations.
Tenant-family audit confirms this is a clean git worktree lane on branch codex/reconcile-platform-ops tracking Dirty13itch/Field_Inspect.
Observed unique value is narrow mobile client API work plus package manifest deltas, not a broader tenant branch.
Keep as preserved lineage/reference only unless the mobile surface becomes active again.

sources.name: Field Inspect postmark build lane
notes: Release-oriented Field Inspect working lane.
Tenant-family audit confirms this is a clean git worktree lane on branch codex/release-52bed54-finish tracking Dirty13itch/Field_Inspect.
Most unique content is deploy output residue and release artifacts that should not be replayed as living tenant truth.
The only surviving narrow code candidate is the Serwist/service-worker route slice; otherwise this lane should freeze into archive evidence.

sources.name: Field Inspect proof tooling lane
notes: Sibling Field Inspect proof-tooling lane.
Tenant-family audit confirms this is a clean git worktree lane on branch codex/reconcile-proof-tooling tracking Dirty13itch/Field_Inspect.
Unique value is bounded to proof scripts, proof tests, and CI helpers rather than a full tenant branch.
Keep as preserved lineage/reference only and mine individual scripts if the primary tenant needs them.

sources.name: Field Inspect proof truth lane
notes: Sibling Field Inspect proof-truth lane.
Tenant-family audit confirms this is a clean git worktree lane on branch codex/reconcile-proof-truth tracking Dirty13itch/Field_Inspect.
Unique value is mostly engineering docs and runbooks such as branch classification, maturity, recovery, and cutover notes rather than active product code.
Keep as lineage/reference truth to mine selectively, then freeze; do not treat it as a co-equal replay lane alongside operations-runtime.

sources.name: RFI & HERS Rater Assistant primary local root
notes: Root-level active workspace discovered by the C drive sweep.
Richer current state than the CodexBuild variants, including dirty field data, docs, and output artifacts.
Tenant-family audit confirms this is the only git-backed workspace in the family, currently dirty on local master with no origin remote configured.
Treat CodexBuild variants as non-git duplicate trees and preservation/archive candidates under this primary root unless they prove unique artifacts.
The plain CodexBuild feature files are now functionally superseded by the root's current namespaced features under src/features/exports, src/features/projects, and src/features/visits.
The duplicate variants are now also governed by the dedicated RFI duplicate-evidence packet and generated report.
The dirty repo-backed root itself is now governed by the dedicated RFI primary-root stabilization packet and generated report.
The broader reconciliation preservation snapshot now captures the root's dirty tracked and untracked surface before later stabilization work mutates it.

A dedicated review worktree at C:/RFI & HERS Rater Assistant-stabilization-review now carries the first four stabilization tranches, and the same branch validated cleanly from the safe mirror path C:/CodexBuild/rfi-hers-stabilization-review because native npm install breaks under the spaced workspace path.

The review-branch-only stabilization fixes for scripts/db-seed.ts, src/features/unit-types/library.ts, and tests/domain-validation.test.ts were landed into C:/RFI & HERS Rater Assistant on 2026-04-07.
The root-level packet validator now passes directly, and the safe-path validation helper remains the governed workaround for the spaced-root Node toolchain issue.

sources.name: Brayburn Trails HOA Website
notes: Top-level product workspace with site, infra, AI handoff, and provider-flexible deployment surfaces.

Classification locked as light-tenant: repo-owned Next.js app, VAULT mirror, Vault secret bridge, Discourse contract, and Terraform contract make it an operator-managed product lane without making Athanor its implementation authority.

sources.name: Ulrich Energy Auditing Website local root
notes: Concrete local working root for the Ulrich energy website family.
Contains website, monitoring, services, and nested app residue beyond the portfolio clone reference.
Current local evidence shows a marketing-site posture with its own hosting and monitoring surfaces but no concrete Athanor dependency.
Classified as standalone-external by default until future work proves a deeper Athanor integration requirement.

sources.name: Amanda Med Tracker local root
notes: Concrete local working root for amanda-med-tracker discovered by the C drive sweep.
Static PWA with Netlify build, local-storage persistence, and no Athanor dependency surface.
Classified as standalone-external while remaining in the governed tenant inventory.

sources.name: Sabrina Ulrich Counseling local root
notes: Top-level counseling website root with active Claude and deployment residue.
Static counseling website with Netlify/Vercel deployment config and no concrete Athanor dependency.
Classified as standalone-external by default while remaining governed in the ecosystem inventory.

sources.name: RFI HERS Rater Assistant
notes: Prototype variant of the root-level RFI & HERS Rater Assistant workspace.
Tenant-family audit confirms this is a non-git duplicate tree rather than a separate repo-backed lane.
Its extra feature files are functionally superseded by the root workspace's current namespaced feature tree, so this variant is now preserved archive evidence rather than an active tenant candidate.
Retain only as duplicate evidence for the 4 KB SQLite seed and the older drizzle migration snapshot.
The exact archive-evidence set is now governed by the dedicated RFI duplicate-evidence packet.

sources.name: RFI HERS Rater Assistant Safe
notes: Safety or fallback variant under the root-level RFI workspace family.
Tenant-family audit confirms this is a non-git duplicate tree rather than a separate repo-backed lane.
Observed unique value is limited to empty-build-check SQLite scratch artifacts and duplicate tiny database seeds.
Keep as preserved archive evidence only; no active product or schema lane remains here.
The exact archive-evidence set is now governed by the dedicated RFI duplicate-evidence packet.

sources.name: RFI HERS Rater Assistant v2
notes: Likely successor variant under the root-level RFI workspace family.
